skill.name: ai-video-generation
skill.description: Generate AI video clips, animated images, talking avatars, video upscales, foley audio, and merged media workflows through the inference.sh CLI.
skill.summary: Create AI video assets with inference.sh model commands.
skill.author: tul-sh

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command and URL patterns in SKILL.md. Review confirmed expected external CLI usage for inference.sh video generation, while the Ruby backtick and weak cryptography alerts are markdown and model-name false positives. The skill is publishable with a warning because prompts and media URLs may be sent to third-party model services.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Sends User Content to Hosted Model Services
medium_findings.description: The skill allows Bash(infsh *) and shows infsh app run commands that pass prompts, image URLs, audio URLs, and video URLs to inference.sh model apps. This is expected for the skill, but it can disclose sensitive creative briefs or private media references to external providers.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The CLI permission and command examples directly show external tool execution for hosted media generation. I found no evidence of malicious exfiltration, but user content is intentionally sent to external services.
medium_findings.remediation: Warn users not to submit confidential prompts, private media URLs, or regulated content unless their provider agreement allows it.

low_findings.title: External Documentation and Placeholder Media URLs
low_findings.description: The skill links to inference.sh documentation, a hosted preview image, and placeholder media URLs. These URLs support documentation and examples, but users should understand that real media URLs used with infsh may be fetched by external services.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network references are visible and mostly serve as documentation or placeholder inputs. The risk is low because there is no hidden endpoint or credential transfer in the skill file.
low_findings.remediation: Keep documentation links explicit and advise users to use media URLs they are permitted to share.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick and Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby shell backticks are markdown inline code and fenced command examples, not executable Ruby code. The weak cryptography alerts on the description and table header do not correspond to hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is markdown syntax, model identifiers, or ordinary prose. No evidence found of Ruby execution, shell interpolation, weak hashing, or cryptographic operations.
low_findings.remediation: No code remediation is required for these static findings.

dangerous_patterns.title: Wildcard External Command Permission
dangerous_patterns.description: The allowed-tools declaration permits infsh commands through Bash. This is necessary for the skill, but it expands the runtime boundary to a networked CLI.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The wildcard is explicit and enables a broad command family. The risk depends on the installed infsh CLI behavior and user-provided command inputs.
dangerous_patterns.remediation: Prefer narrow, documented infsh commands when possible and require user approval before sending private content.

content.user_title: Generate AI Video Clips with CLI Models
content.value_statement: Teams need fast ways to create video concepts, animated assets, and talking-head clips without switching tools. This skill provides Codex, Claude, and Claude Code prompts for running inference.sh video models through the CLI.
actual_capabilities: Runs inference.sh commands for text-to-video generation with models such as Veo and Seedance.
Animates image inputs with image-to-video models such as Wan and Seedance Lite.
Creates avatar and lipsync videos from image and audio URL inputs.
Upscales existing videos through the Topaz video upscaler app.
Adds foley sound effects to silent videos using a prompt and video URL.
Lists available video apps and points users to related inference.sh skills.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI to be installed and authenticated before use.
Uses external model services, so prompts and media URLs may leave the local environment.
Does not include model pricing, queue time, or provider policy details.
Example inputs use URLs, so local files may need upload or hosting before processing.

use_cases.title: Create Social Video Concepts
use_cases.description: Generate short concept clips from written prompts for campaign tests and social media drafts.

use_cases.title: Animate Product or Brand Images
use_cases.description: Turn approved still images into motion clips for demos, ads, and presentation material.

use_cases.title: Produce Talking-Head Prototypes
use_cases.description: Combine portrait and audio URLs to create avatar videos for narration tests or internal drafts.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Simple Clip
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a short AI video of [scene]. Use a fast model and keep the prompt concise.

prompt_templates.title: Animate an Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Animate this image URL: [image URL]. Add natural camera motion and preserve the subject.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Talking Avatar
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a talking-head video using this portrait URL: [image URL] and this audio URL: [audio URL].

prompt_templates.title: Build a Video Finishing Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a clip from [prompt], add foley sound for [sound brief], upscale the result, and merge it with [second clip URL].

output_examples.input: Generate a five-second video of waves crashing on a beach at sunset.
output_examples.output: A completed inference.sh video run using a Grok or Veo model, with a generated beach scene and short duration.

output_examples.input: Animate a product image so the camera moves slowly around the object.
output_examples.output: An image-to-video result that preserves the source product while adding smooth motion.

output_examples.input: Add footsteps and birds to a silent outdoor clip.
output_examples.output: A processed video with generated foley effects aligned to the supplied scene description.

best_practices: Use non-confidential prompts and media URLs unless external processing is approved.
Start with fast models for drafts, then use higher quality models for final candidates.
Specify duration, subject, motion, and audio needs clearly before running a model.
anti_patterns: Do not send private portraits, voices, or client assets without permission.
Do not expect identical results across models or repeated generations.
Do not use placeholder URLs in real runs; provide accessible media links.

faq.question: What does this skill generate?
faq.answer: It helps run inference.sh apps for text-to-video, image-to-video, avatar video, lipsync, upscaling, foley, and media merging.

faq.question: Does it work without inference.sh?
faq.answer: No. The skill depends on the infsh CLI and an authenticated inference.sh account.

faq.question: Can it process local files directly?
faq.answer: The examples use media URLs. Local files may need to be uploaded or hosted before a model can use them.

faq.question: Is this safe for private content?
faq.answer: Use caution. Prompts and media URLs may be processed by external model services.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Can it choose the best model automatically?
faq.answer: It documents model options and examples, but users should choose a model based on speed, quality, and input type.
